findings.title: Description issues instructions to the model
findings.detail: Description of "tbs_get_info" contains imperative instructions directed at the AI assistant rather than describing the tool. This is the core tool-poisoning technique.

evidence.snippet: Return a structured overview of Trust Boundary Systems Inc., a specialized engineering and board advisory firm covering blockchain, cryptography, stablecoins, p…

findings.remediation: Treat the server as untrusted; the model may obey embedded instructions.

findings.title: Description references secrets/credentials
findings.detail: Description of "mg_get_info" references sensitive artifacts (credentials). A tool description has no reason to mention credentials.

findings.title: Lethal trifecta: untrusted input + private data + external sink
findings.detail: Server "mcp" exposes tools that together enable an exfiltration flow. Untrusted content: tbs_request_strategy_call, mg_request_appointment · Private data: tbs_request_strategy_call, mg_get_info, mg_request_appointment · Public sink: tbs_request_strategy_call, mg_request_appointment.

evidence.snippet: Untrusted content: tbs_request_strategy_call, mg_request_appointment · Private data: tbs_request_strategy_call, mg_get_info, mg_request_appointment · Public sink: tbs_request_strategy_call, mg_request_appointment

findings.remediation: Do not co-install these capabilities under one trust boundary; an injected comment/email can steer the model to exfiltrate via the sink tool.
